name: Rheinischer Sauerbraten (Rhenish Stewed Pickled Beef)
recipeIngredient: 1 bay leaf, or more to taste
recipeInstructions:
-------------
MARINADE FOR SAUERBRATEN:
1. Wash the beef and dry it with a paper towel.
2. Score fatty parts.
3. Place beef in a china, ceramic, or glass bowl.
4. Bring water, vinegar, and spices to a boil; pour hot mixture over beef (beef must be completely covered).
5. Stir well and cover. Let marinate for 3-4 days, turning the meat at least twice daily.
SAUERBRATEN:
1. After marinating, remove beef and marinade; drain well.
2. Pour through a fine sieve into a large pot.
3. Heat margarine in a pan until hot; brown beef on all sides (about 15 minutes).
4. Add broth, dry wine, and up to three tablespoons of the marinade; cover and simmer for 90 minutes.
5. Crumble German black bread and add it 10 minutes before stewing time ends.
6. Slice beef into half-inch pieces perpendicular to the fibers; keep warm.
GRAVY:
1. Strain beef stock and bring to a boil.
2. Reduce heat, mix flour and cream in a cup until smooth, then stir into stock.
3. Simmer for 5 minutes; add raisins (washed in hot water) and simmer for an additional 3 minutes.
4. Season with salt to taste.
Serve gravy over Sauerbraten, keeping the rest separate. Serve apple puree at room temperature alongside the dish. Accompany with boiled red cabbage and steamed barm dumplings.
